scaffolds.q: The meninges can become infected by bacteria.
scaffolds.explain: Bacterial meningitis occurs when bacteria (Streptococcus pneumoniae, Neisseria meningitidis, Listeria) breach the meninges and multiply in the CSF. Cast your attention here:: the immune system floods the subarachnoid space with neutrophils, inflammation damages tissue, and intracranial pressure rises. This is a medical emergency.

scaffolds.q: What is the normal white blood cell count in cerebrospinal fluid?
scaffolds.explain: Normal CSF contains 0-5 WBCs per microliter — essentially sterile. Finding hundreds or thousands indicates infection or inflammation. Allow your mind to embrace this:what that number means: the immune system has recognized a threat inside your CNS and is flooding the scene with defenders.
